text: When Russia attacked Ukraine in 2022, the world was reminded that there is always controversy concerning the practices of intense warfare at the interstate level in the modern era. Views as to the likelihood and nature of war tend to evolve with the passage of time and events. Since 2014 Ukraine has been battling the aggressions of the Russian state and this study provides a systematic evaluation of the first year of warfare resulting from Russia's invasion of Ukraine in 2022. As in previous years, this study primarily utilizes the analytic construct of 'resilient fighting power' (RFP). As a theoretical reference frame for all issues concerning warfare and its various dimensions, RFP explains the doctrinal underpinnings, theory, development, training, operational procedures, and various structural and commodity components, which integrate a unit of armed forces to successfully conduct combat and achieve victory at all levels. Throughout history, strategy for armies reflects doctrinal and ideological traditions and concepts that are established through the institutional processes that train command personnel as effective professionals. The Armed Forces of Ukraine (AFU) and the Russian Armed Forces evolved strategically as institutions for carrying out war by reference to their Soviet-derived roots and historical doctrinal traditions over a prolonged time. However, by participating in warfare against Russia over a year that spanned 14 months from the day of the start of the full-scale invasion, the AFU as a military organisation appeared to adopt or attempt to incorporate aspects of the doctrine and NATO-orientated institutional procedures that mark a change of its own from its earlier Soviet-inherited military traditions to a very different operational approach than it is supposed to have adopted since 2014. The RFP concept facilitates not only the objective measurement and development of armed forces that fight in various types of combat within the broad context of war; but can provide practical evidence of whether civilian society and its economy was truly capable and has sufficient strength or resiliency so as to enable armed forces to survive and therefore act effectively throughout all stages of their operational campaign to cause the enemy defeat and gain the victory that will assure national success and total victory that include an occupiers defeat and political gains for the country under siege, while describing various instances of armed foreign intervention through programmes of assistance. Thus, in the context of Ukrainian state power's struggles to modernize, progress towards achieving the key component of a state's survival capabilities that would facilitate progress toward strong institutions -- at all levels -- an economy that works for all; effective, competent armed forces; adherence to and a stable set of internationally recognized rules of law for interactions; a global leadership role; efficient information distribution methods through unbiased media, including free-flowing access to all sources on the global wide-web for every member of society; free, universal access to knowledge to raise the education level and competence for societal and working existence of all its citizens; protection of the national culture and peace of every citizen based upon protection and restoration of the natural environment and for the promotion of ecologically- sustainable activities and policy toward building more and better living space; while it is also concerning protection of civilians abroad; and protection of a society under siege from enemy forces, this study found the unification and inter-relation between institutional and policy transformation and the capacity of a society to endure crises as being most relevant in explaining Ukrainian Armed Forces warfighting resilience that ultimately determined their operational success -- against the initial attacks that were intense; that provided extraordinary support to Russian separatist troops in the conflict zones in Eastern Ukraine through heavy weapon strikes including powerful heavy artillery and the various types of airpower on all sides -- with local residents reporting very severe falling pressure in their pipes due to these bombardments being frequently carried out for months on end by numerous forces both on land and at sea in the war of nerves that prepared for and accompanied hostilities the initiation and progress of the full-scale war; and even the counter offensive actions, which signified improvement to armed forces' capacities in respect to their ability to apply power within all combat arms through information, weaponry that was highly accurate, the latest technologies, while taking into consideration other non-military warfare instruments like disinformation and the effects of the cyber attacks of international aggressors that both threatened Ukraines civil defense with destructive consequences while having the potential to undermine vital processes of life that society and people took for granted including their communication links -- with respect to the impacts that foreign weapons aided to have a great deal in modernizing the AFU's ability to carry out war in order to ultimately gain Russian state sovereignty undermining as well as with the various implications that the themes explored in the book would have in contemporary world security as well as the studies of warfare and the role that armed struggle may yet have for centuries to come, especially in regard to understanding and the development of various military forces.
